overview: Removes inline codes (or parts of inline codes) from the text units of a document. Inline codes are spans of data that should be left untouched inside translatable text, such as `<b>` tags in HTML paragraphs. The step offers three removal modes: stripping markers only, stripping content only, or stripping both entirely.

stripSource.help: Applies the selected removal action to the **source** text of each text unit. When disabled, source inline codes are left intact regardless of the removal mode chosen.

stripTarget.help: Applies the selected removal action to the **target** text of each text unit. When disabled, target inline codes are left intact regardless of the removal mode chosen.

mode.help:
An inline code has two parts: **markers** (always present) and **content** (optional). For example, in XLIFF:

```xml
<bpt id="1"><b></bpt>
```

- `<bpt id="1">...</bpt>` = marker
- `<b>` = content

The three modes control which parts are stripped.
mode.values:
Integer selecting the removal mode:
- **0** — Remove code **marker**, but keep code content (content becomes part of the text)
- **1** — Remove code **content**, but keep code marker (code is generalized; original format data is lost)
- **2** — Remove code **marker and content** (entire inline code is removed with no trace)

includeNonTranslatable.help: Controls whether the removal action is also applied to text units marked as non-translatable. If disabled, all non-translatable text units are left completely untouched.

replaceWithSpace.help:
Replaces line-break inline codes with a space character instead of removing them entirely. A code qualifies as a line-break code if its type is `Code.TYPE_LB` or its content contains any of these expressions (case-insensitive): `
`, `
`, `
`, `\n`, `\r`, `\u0085`, `\u2028`, `\u2029`
notes: This option **only applies** when `mode` is set to **2** (Remove code marker and code content). It has no effect in other modes.

dependsOn.condition: Only effective when mode = 2 (remove marker and content)

examples.title: Strip all inline codes from XLIFF source
examples.description: Completely removes inline codes (markers + content) from the source text, replacing line-break codes with spaces to preserve word boundaries.
examples.config:
```yaml
stripSource: true
stripTarget: false
mode: 2
replaceWithSpace: true
includeNonTranslatable: true
```

examples.title: Generalize inline codes (strip content only)
examples.description: Removes the format-specific content from inline codes while preserving their markers. This generalizes the codes so they no longer carry original format data, which can be useful for format-neutral processing.
examples.config:
```yaml
stripSource: true
stripTarget: true
mode: 1
includeNonTranslatable: false
```

processingNotes: The step operates on filter events and passes modified filter events downstream.
Both source and target stripping are enabled by default — disable explicitly if you only want to strip one side.
